title: Baumiella imbricata
taxonomy: Baumiella imbricata is classified within the family Cladoniaceae, which is a group of lichens commonly known as 'reindeer lichens'. This classification is based on the species' morphological and anatomical characteristics. Within the family Cladoniaceae, Baumiella imbricata is assigned to the genus Baumiella, a group of lichens that are typically found in high-latitude and high-elevation environments. The genus Baumiella is further divided into several species, with Baumiella imbricata being one of the recognized species within this group. The classification of Baumiella imbricata is an ongoing area of research, and new discoveries may lead to further refinements in its classification.
